title: Manifesto In Motion
definition: The idea that a philosophical conversation can be visualized as an animated short film to inspire and provoke thought.

difficulty_levels.beginner: A creative way of sharing ideas in the form of an animated short movie. Like watching a story come alive.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: Using animation as a medium to express complex philosophical concepts in a captivating and engaging manner, inviting the audience to reflect upon them.
difficulty_levels.expert: An innovative approach that combines art, philosophy, and technology by using animated short films as manifestos capable of disseminating ideas, sparking discussions, and fostering intellectual growth in a visual, accessible, and emotionally impactful way.

extra_fields.insight: The potential for animated films to serve as living philosophical dialogues, inviting viewers to explore complex concepts through captivating storytelling and visual aesthetics.
